channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Melania Trump to debut documentary with Amazon Prime Video
item.description: Incoming first lady Melania Trump will star in a documentary film project with Amazon Prime Video set to debut in the second half of 2025, an Amazon spokesperson announced Sunday. Filming for the documentary began in December 2024 and is ongoing, according to the press release.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Stay Safe Online: The Rise of Fake VPN Apps and How to Protect Yourself
item.description: The number of users encountering fake VPN apps has jumped significantly, but you can stay safe by following these essential steps.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok creators left in limbo while awaiting decision on potential platform ban
item.description: The fate of TikTok is keeping creators and small business owners in anxious limbo as they await a decision from the Supreme Court that could upheld their livelihoods.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr. And Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NASA's Martian helicopter promises unprecedented views of the red planet
item.description: NASA's Ingenuity helicopter, which flew on Mars in April 2021, became the first aircraft to achieve powered, controlled flight on a planet other than Earth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 hidden reasons you keep running out of iCloud storage
item.description: Apple gives every iCloud user 5GB of free storage space. This storage space can be used for anything—from backing up your iPhone online to saving documents in the cloud to syncing photos across devices.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Ditch boring emoji and create your own unique versions with Genmoji on iPhone
item.description: Genmoji, the latest addition to Apple's Apple Intelligence suite, uses artificial intelligence to generate customized emoji based on text descriptions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Net neutrality never stood a chance
item.description: A federal appeals court sealed the fate of the Biden administration’s efforts to regulate internet providers on Thursday, striking down the Federal Communications Commission’s 2024 net neutrality order once and for all.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Dating Sunday 2025: The busiest day on dating apps is almost here
item.description: Dating apps are gearing up for their busiest day of the year: Dating Sunday. This landmark day in the dating world always lands on the first Sunday of January.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Court Blocks Reinstatement of Net Neutrality Rules
item.description: A US appeals court blocks efforts by the FCC to reinstate net neutrality rules, casting doubt on their reintroduction for the next five years.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why Apple TV+ is offering a free weekend of binge-watching
item.description: Apple TV+ is hoping people will make a dent in the Strategic Popcorn Reserve by bingeing its streaming TV and movies for free this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Everything new on Paramount Plus in January 2025
item.description: Escape the January blues with a whole new slate of movies and TV shows coming to Paramount Plus this month.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple needs good AI acquisition hires
item.description: When it comes to artificial intelligence development, Apple seems more interested than usual in seeking out good acquisition targets, potentially building on the small acquisitions it makes most months. The company has, in fact, led the industry when it comes to strategic AI acquisitions since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: VPNs are disappearing from India's app stores – and a 2022 law may be the culprit
item.description: The VPN removal orders appear to be the first enactment of the controversial 2022 India's data retention law. Here's what we know so far.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: US PC prices could soar if Trump tariff threats come true
item.description: Proposed import tariffs for the US could see PC costs rise as much as 46%, with smartphones also becoming more expensive.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple settles Siri eavesdropping accusations for $95M
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging the tech giant used its voice assistant Siri to obtain and share private conversations without users' permission.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Turo rentals emerge as common thread in Las Vegas Cybertruck and New Orleans deadly incidents
item.description: Turo faces scrutiny after tragic incidents involving rented vehicles, raising questions about security measures and regulatory frameworks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple denies wrongdoing but settles to pay $95 million cash (about $20 per class member) in Siri privacy suit
item.description: Apple agreed to pay $95 million in cash to settle a proposed class action lawsuit claiming that its voice-activated Siri assistant violated users’ privacy. A preliminary settlement was filed on Tuesday night in the Oakland, California federal court, and requires approval by U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New rules may ban Chinese drones if approved by Trump administration
item.description: The U.S. Commerce Department said on Thursday it is considering new rules that would impose restrictions on Chinese drones that would restrict or ban them in the United States citing national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Easy internet sleuth tricks you’ll wish you knew sooner
item.description: Here are some powerful online sleuthing tricks you can use to check the credibility of a website, see old pictures of places you've lived and even learn more about your neighbors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024
item.description: Current and former federal technology executives weighed in on what topics drove the storylines in 2024. The post No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Health AI: How Apple can boost public health
item.description: Combined with its existing solutions, Apple’s strategic approach to artificial intelligence (AI) deployment could make a radical difference to public health. Here is how it could achieve that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI, power availability and Intel’s future
item.description: As 2025 kicks off, it’s time to dust off the crystal ball. Here are my data center predictions for the coming year. 1) Trump backs off on tech tariffs For all the handwringing, Trump has a history of not following through on his threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk defends demonetizing critics' content on X
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk defended his move to demonetize his critics' content on his social platform X and maintained the decision did not violate free speech. "The first amendment is protection for 'free speech', not 'paid speech' ffs," Musk wrote Wednesday on X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Confronting the growing drone threat
item.description: There is a solid rationale for prioritizing safe, secure and controlled RF cyber-based counter-UAS technology as decision makers consider future standards. The post Confronting the growing drone threat first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: IA generativa en 2025: de la experimentación a la práctica real
item.description: Parece llegar a su fin el tiempo de que las empresas jueguen con la IA generativa: las organizaciones comienzan una nueva etapa en la que se reduce la experimentación para pivotar hacia logros de valor empresarial, con un enfoque en menos casos de uso pero más específicos.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What is Turo? Car rental app used in New Orleans and Las Vegas
item.description: Turo, a San Francisco-based company founded in 2010, operates differently from traditional car rental services.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: The US government must keep pace with serving an AI-empowered citizenry
item.description: Our government needs to maintain trust through competence, and that competence will increasingly be about powering government services with AI. The post The US government must keep pace with serving an AI-empowered citizenry first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Intro to VSCode.dev: The IDE in your browser
item.description: The fully realized browser-based IDE has been a long time coming. Ever since the beginning of the cloud revolution, I’ve thought a powerful and flexible remote IDE was just around the corner. I imagined a development tool that could use any virtual machine as a development platform.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Revealed: The 10 most popular and worst passwords of 2024
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson cites a new report that says many people still use weak passwords like "123456," risking their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to use Gemini AI to make templates in Google Sheets
item.description: Need ideas or motivation to help you build a spreadsheet in Google Sheets? You can browse through the templates that are included in this office app and select one to customize. But a more intriguing option is to use the tool in Sheets called Help Me Organize.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Homeland Security helps write the book on how to acquire artificial intelligence
item.description: Few departments have more diverse procurement needs than Homeland Security. The agency operates on natural disasters, immigration activity and cybersecurity. The post Homeland Security helps write the book on how to acquire artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network
